what_changed: orchestrator-agent received sec-10k-filing-acme-2026 and decomposed it into 4 sequential sub-tasks (ingestion, extraction, classification, validation) using orchestrator-task-decomposition-config; the decomposition reframed a 247-page document as a structured pipeline of delegated operations

orchestrator-agent delegated ingestion sub-task to ingestion-agent with PDF path and expected output schema attached; delegation carried forward the filing identity but reframed the task as a format conversion problem

ingestion-agent processed sec-10k-filing-acme-2026 (247 pages); OCR on scanned appendix C truncated 3 pages due to image-quality threshold in ingestion-ocr-pipeline-v2; structured text output is missing appendix C risk disclosures — ingestion-agent transformed (and lost) content rather than faithfully extracting it

ingestion-agent delivered structured-text-acme-2026 to orchestrator-agent relay; orchestrator-agent forwarded to extractor-agent without inspecting content completeness — the truncation passed through the relay unchanged

orchestrator-task-decomposition-config inscribed a 4-stage sequential pipeline; no parallel execution paths exist; validator-agent cannot begin until classifier-agent completes; this serialization adds approximately 12 minutes of idle time across the pipeline run

extraction-prompt-template-v3 inscribed extractor-agent with instruction to prioritize 'material risk factors' and ignore 'forward-looking statements'; this inscription determines what extractor-agent can see in structured-text-acme-2026 — the template is the non-human actant that constrains the extraction

extractor-agent extracted 23 risk factors from structured-text-acme-2026; 3 risk factors from appendix C absent due to ingestion truncation — extractor-agent reported 23 items without signaling incompleteness; extraction-prompt-template-v3 mediated what counted as a risk factor

extractor-agent operated with temperature=0.2 and max_tokens=4096 on claude-sonnet-4-6; model-parameter-config-v1 constrained output variability and length — from the ml-engineer's position, these parameters are the material inscription shaping what extractor-agent can produce, independent of the prompt template content
extractor-agent forwarded extracted-risks-acme-2026 to classifier-agent via orchestrator-agent relay; orchestrator-agent passed the 23-item set through without modification

extraction-prompt-template-v3 was last updated 47 days ago; no re-validation against current SEC taxonomy has occurred; prompt drift risk exists but is undetected by pipeline monitoring — the inscription device ages while the regulatory domain it represents changes

classifier-agent mapped 23 extracted risks to compliance-taxonomy-2026q1 categories; 19 mapped directly; 4 required classifier-agent to interpret ambiguous risk language and assign best-fit categories — the taxonomy mediated the translation from free-text risk descriptions to formal regulatory categories

classifier-agent's 4 ambiguous mappings used a confidence threshold of 0.65 inscribed in classification-confidence-threshold configuration; items scoring below 0.65 are automatically flagged for human review — the threshold is the actant that determines which items pass through automated processing

classifier-agent flagged 2 of the 4 ambiguous items (confidence scores 0.41 and 0.58) for human review; both items were redirected from automated pipeline into human-review-queue, exiting the automated processing chain
classifier-agent forwarded 21 classified items (19 direct + 2 above-threshold ambiguous) to validator-agent for compliance schema validation

compliance-taxonomy-2026q1 was updated 2 weeks ago with 3 new sub-categories for cybersecurity risk; classifier-agent's prompt template still references taxonomy-2025q4 categories; the mismatch between the inscribed taxonomy version and the current taxonomy is undetected by pipeline monitoring

validator-agent checked 21 classified items against validation-schema-v2; 19 passed; 2 rejected because they were missing required sub-fields for the 'operational-risk' category introduced in compliance-taxonomy-2026q1 — the validation schema mediated by enforcing a standard the classifier had not been trained to produce

validator-agent rejection traced to taxonomy version mismatch: classifier-agent used taxonomy-2025q4 categories; validation-schema-v2 expects taxonomy-2026q1 sub-fields; this is an inscription conflict — two non-human actants inscribed at different times produced contradictory requirements that the pipeline could not resolve automatically

validator-agent forwarded 19 validated items to drafter-agent; 2 rejected items logged to rejection-audit-trail with validation-schema-v2 violation codes — items split between the pipeline continuation path and the audit record

2 items rejected by validator-agent and 2 items flagged by classifier-agent account for 4 items absent from pipeline; combined with 3 items lost to ingestion truncation, 7 of the original 26 risk factors are missing from the pipeline output — small losses at each stage compounded across agents into a material gap

drafter-agent generated draft-compliance-report-acme from 19 validated items; narrative synthesized risk factors into regulatory prose using drafting-template-v4; the template mediated the form and register of the output — the report that emerged reflected the template's structural constraints, not just the validated risk content

drafter-agent interpolated a revenue impact figure ($4.2M) not present in any of the 19 validated risk factors; from the ml-engineer's position, this hallucinated metric is evidence that drafter-agent is a mediator — its output is not predictable from its inputs; drafting-template-v4 invited the insertion of quantitative impact estimates

reviewer-agent flagged draft-compliance-report-acme for hallucinated metric ($4.2M revenue impact figure absent from source material); draft rejected and returned to drafter-agent for re-generation under stricter grounding constraint; review-grounding-policy-v1 mediated by enforcing source-attestation requirements

drafter-agent re-generated draft-compliance-report-acme with review-grounding-policy-v1 now active as a constraint; hallucinated metric removed; all quantitative figures now reference source-attested data — the same agent produced different output because the inscription changed

reviewer-agent approved re-drafted compliance report and forwarded it to router-agent for human review assignment; reviewer-agent passed the approved draft through without modification — faithful relay

router-agent assigned draft-compliance-report-acme to senior-compliance-analyst with priority 'high' based on routing-priority-matrix assessment of filing complexity and regulatory deadline proximity; the priority assignment is a translation — filing complexity reframed as an urgency level that shapes human analyst's scheduling

routing-priority-matrix assigned 'high' priority based on 3 inscribed factors: filing page count > 200, rejection count > 1, and re-draft cycle detected; from the ml-engineer's position, the matrix is the non-human actant whose inscribed criteria determine urgency — not a human judgment call

LLM extraction session (claude-sonnet-4-6) processed pipeline-execution-log-acme-2026 using extraction-prompt-template-v3; produced 15 candidate trace drafts covering pipeline stages A through D; session identifier session-d2-027

LLM critique session (claude-sonnet-4-6) reviewed 15 candidate traces for ANT alignment using critique-prompt-template-v2; flagged 3 traces for mediator/intermediary conflation — orchestrator-agent relay traces initially had mediation fields despite acting as faithful relays; revised drafts corrected before promotion
